title: paphiopedilum × holdenii
intro: Paphiopedilum × holdenii, also known as Holden's Paph, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Paphiopedilum victoria-regina and Paphiopedilum victoria-regina var. philippinense. This plant is known to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where its rhizomes are used to treat various health conditions. The rhizomes of Paphiopedilum × holdenii contain alkaloids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. In herbal preparations, the rhizomes are often dried and crushed to be used in teas or tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation.
